notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix → /nw-deliver. Authoritative spec is the RCA at `docs/feature/fix-cgroup-subtree-control-delegation/bugfix-rca.md` (user-approved 2026-05-09 in /nw-bugfix Phase 2 with BROAD scope before roadmap creation). The fix shape, production code sites, regression test shape, and broad-scope cleanup scope are all settled in the RCA — the roadmap formalizes that decomposition; it does not redesign the fix.

Two phases, four steps. Phase 01 is the production fix + regression test as a single vertical slice (RED → GREEN). Phase 02 is the broad-scope mechanical cleanup (test migration off TempDir + dead-code removal). Phase 02 depends on Phase 01 because the new `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` init from 01-02 is the prerequisite real-cgroupfs bootstrap surface the migrated tests call.

Regression test home: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/` (NOT the worker crate). Rationale: the test boots BOTH `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` AND `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` and drives `ExecDriver::start` through to the real cgroupfs scope — the cross-crate orchestration shape that mirrors the production `overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:436` wiring lives here, not in the worker crate's per-driver test surface. The existing `cgroup_isolation/` directory and its `AllocCleanup`-style guard precedent at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/cleanup.rs` confirm this is the established home for cross-crate cgroup-bootstrap integration tests.

Single-cut migration per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Single-cut migrations in greenfield': Phase 02's first step replaces TempDir scaffolding with real `/sys/fs/cgroup` bootstrapping in one cohesive commit per file (or one commit for all 10 files; crafter's call). Phase 02's second step deletes the now-unreachable `tempdir-as-cgroupfs` ENOTEMPTY fallback in `remove_workload_scope` plus the `is_dir_not_empty` helper if no other callers remain. Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Deletion discipline', any test that exists ONLY to cover that deleted branch is removed in the same commit — not gated, not salvaged.

Cgroup-write order is load-bearing (RCA § Production fix #1). `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` MUST write `+cpu +memory +io +pids` to `overdrive.slice/cgroup.subtree_control` BEFORE creating `control-plane.slice` underneath OR enrolling the server PID. A cgroup with a process in `cgroup.procs` cannot have controllers added to its parent's `subtree_control` — the kernel returns EBUSY. Same ordering invariant applies to `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers`: enable controllers on `workloads.slice/cgroup.subtree_control` BEFORE creating any `alloc-*.scope` underneath.

Idempotency under repeated boot is required. Both `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` and `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` must be idempotent (a second call on already-enabled `subtree_control` is a no-op; the kernel re-confirms enablement). Crafter's tests must verify this — boot twice, assert second call returns Ok with no observable side effects. This is the path that makes the fix safe against systemd-managed `overdrive.slice` deployments where systemd has already set up delegation.

Typed errors per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Distinct failure modes get distinct error variants': the `subtree_control` write surfaces as discrete typed-error variants. Distinguish at minimum `SubtreeControlBusy` (kernel returned EBUSY — slice was previously initialised in wrong order; operator hint: restart cleanly) from `SubtreeControlWriteFailed` (any other I/O error — operator hint: inspect cgroupfs delegation for the enclosing slice). Do NOT absorb into a generic `io::Error`. Crafter to extend `CgroupBootstrapError` (or equivalent — name follows existing convention in the touched crate) rather than introduce a parallel error type.

Tests run through `cargo xtask lima run --` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Cgroup writes need root or delegation' and § 'Running tests — Lima VM'. Compile-only `cargo nextest run --no-run` on macOS remains the dev-workstation pre-push gate; runtime gate is Linux via Lima. Mutation invocation: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package <crate> --file <changed-files>` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Mutation testing (cargo-mutants)' → 'Usage' (Lima required because integration-tests feature is involved).

Per-step mutation testing per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Per-step vs per-PR scoping'. Phase 01's GREEN step (01-02) runs the per-step `--file`-scoped mutation gate; Phase 02's deletion step (02-02) does the same. Phase 02's test-migration step (02-01) is test-only with no production logic change — skip mutation; the per-PR final gate catches anything missed. Kill-rate gate ≥80% on the touched files.

`#[serial(cgroup)]` annotation required on the new regression test per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Tests that mutate process-global state'. The test mutates real `/sys/fs/cgroup` (process-global state) and must serialise within the binary; using an explicit `cgroup` group key (rather than bare `#[serial]` or `#[serial(env)]`) lets unrelated env-mutating tests still run in parallel. Migrated `exec_driver/*` tests in 02-01 carry the same annotation for the same reason. The `serial_test` workspace dev-dep is already declared per project convention.

`AllocCleanup`-style RAII guard required for every test that creates an alloc scope under real `/sys/fs/cgroup`. Pattern lives at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/cleanup.rs::AllocCleanup`: `Drop` impl fires `cgroup.kill` (cgroup v2 mass-kill) then `rmdir` on the scope directory so leaked workloads do not survive the test process exit. Required by `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Leaked workload cgroups across runs' — the rule's preventive surface. Phase 02's test-migration step inherits the same guard at every alloc creation site.

Out of scope per RCA § 'What this RCA does NOT cover' — do NOT touch: (a) ADR-0026 D9 disposition (warn-and-continue policy stays unchanged; if crafter spots an obvious site where EACCES specifically should escalate, surface as a follow-up to the user via a deferral message — do NOT create a GH issue unilaterally per `feedback_no_unilateral_gh_issues`, do NOT change the disposition without explicit user sign-off); (b) cgroup preflight (`crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_preflight.rs`) — already correct after the four prior `fix-cgroup-preflight-*` features; (c) `--allow-no-cgroups` flag (already removed per ADR-0034); (d) `CONTROL_PLANE_SLICE` constant (path is correct); (e) systemd-unit production deployments (fix is idempotent against them); (f) `overdrive.slice/control-plane.slice` does NOT need its own `subtree_control` write (the control-plane process does not create child cgroups under itself).

Effort/time estimates are advisory per CLAUDE.md § 'No effort/time budget cuts' — the crafter lands the full work each step's AC describes, however long it takes. Clippy issues surfaced during a step's quality gate are in-scope fixes per CLAUDE.md § 'Deferrals require GitHub issues' / `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer` — they are NOT deferrals. Deferrals require explicit user approval BEFORE any GH issue creation per `feedback_no_unilateral_gh_issues` (incident #161).

`force_limit_write_failure` injection seam at `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs:291-296` stays. The seam fires synthetic EACCES regardless of the underlying filesystem, so it is insensitive to the TempDir → real-cgroupfs migration; verify `limit_write_failure_warns.rs` continues to assert the warn-and-continue path after the migration in 02-01.

phases.name: Production fix + regression test (RED → GREEN)
user_stories: As an operator running `overdrive serve` on a host with cgroup v2 + delegation correctly configured on the enclosing slice, an alloc with `cpu_milli=2000, memory_bytes=128MiB` actually has `cpu.weight=200` and `memory.max=134217728` written to its `alloc-*.scope` cgroup directory (instead of the silent warn-and-continue per ADR-0026 D9 that leaves the workload running unisolated).
phases.description: Land the production fix (delegation of `+cpu +memory +io +pids` to `overdrive.slice/cgroup.subtree_control` and `+cpu +memory` to `overdrive.slice/workloads.slice/cgroup.subtree_control`) plus the new real-cgroupfs regression test. Two-step Outside-In TDD: 01-01 introduces the new typed-error variants and the failing regression test (RED scaffold); 01-02 implements the two `subtree_control` writes in their owning crates, wires the worker init into the production bootstrap path, and transitions the regression test RED → GREEN.

steps.name: RED — typed-error variants + failing regression test
steps.description: Land the new `CgroupBootstrapError::SubtreeControlBusy { source: io::Error }` and `CgroupBootstrapError::SubtreeControlWriteFailed { source: io::Error }` variants in whichever crate owns the bootstrap surface (extend the existing `CgroupBootstrapError` enum if present; otherwise introduce one in `overdrive-control-plane` per existing convention — name and home follow the precedent of the prior `fix-cgroup-preflight-*` features). Production code in `cgroup_manager.rs` is UNCHANGED in this commit — neither `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` nor any new `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` is touched yet; the variants are added but not yet constructed by any production call site. Land the new RED regression test `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/alloc_scope_has_writable_cpu_weight_and_memory_max.rs` exactly per the RCA § 'Regression test' shape: `#[serial(cgroup)]`, boots both inits against real `/sys/fs/cgroup`, registers an `AllocCleanup` guard, drives `ExecDriver::start` for an alloc with `cpu_milli=2000, memory_bytes=128MiB`, asserts `cpu.weight=200` AND `memory.max=134217728`. Plus the companion log-capture assertion that the WARN line `cgroup resource-limit write failed` did NOT fire on the success path (use existing `tracing-subscriber` test capture or `tracing_test` if present in the workspace; if neither, surface as a blocker to the user). Wire both new test files through `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration.rs` inside the existing inline `mod cgroup_isolation { ... }` block. Mark the RED scaffold per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'RED scaffolds': `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]` on the test body, with a `panic!("Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold (subtree_control delegation regression)")` at the top so it transitions naturally when 01-02 lands. Conventional commit shape; lefthook should pass cleanly with the `should_panic` shape — do NOT use `--no-verify`.
criteria: AC1: `CgroupBootstrapError` (extended or newly introduced) carries at minimum two new variants: `SubtreeControlBusy { source: io::Error }` (with rustdoc naming the EBUSY-on-already-enrolled-pid kernel response and the operator hint 'restart the server cleanly') and `SubtreeControlWriteFailed { source: io::Error }` (with rustdoc naming the catch-all I/O failure mode and the operator hint 'inspect cgroupfs delegation for the enclosing slice'). Both variants follow `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Errors' (thiserror, `#[from]` where appropriate, constructor methods if the existing enum convention has them).

AC2: New test file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/alloc_scope_has_writable_cpu_weight_and_memory_max.rs` exists with the shape from the RCA § 'Regression test' (boots `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` + `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers`, drives `ExecDriver::start` for an alloc with `cpu_milli=2000, memory_bytes=128MiB`, asserts `cpu.weight=200`, `memory.max=134217728`). Annotated `#[serial(cgroup)]`. Marked as RED scaffold via `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]` + a `panic!` at the top of the body per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Test-side scaffolds'.

AC3: New companion test (in the same file or a sibling file at the same path) asserts that the WARN log line `cgroup resource-limit write failed` did NOT fire during the success path. Uses the workspace's existing tracing-test mechanism; if no such mechanism exists in the workspace, the crafter MUST surface this as a blocker to the user before introducing a new dependency. Marked as RED scaffold same as AC2.

AC4: Both new test files are wired through `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration.rs` via `mod alloc_scope_has_writable_cpu_weight_and_memory_max;` (and any companion file's mod line) inside the existing inline `mod cgroup_isolation { ... }` block.

AC5: Production source files `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs` and `crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs` are UNMODIFIED by this commit (verify via `git diff --stat HEAD` — only the new error variants and test wiring should appear in the diff against HEAD). The wiring site at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:436` is also UNMODIFIED.

AC6: Compile-only gate `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests --no-run` (and `-p overdrive-worker` if the new error variant lives there) succeeds on the dev workstation. Inside Lima, `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` shows the new tests passing as `should_panic` (the panic message matches the expected substring), all other tests continue to pass.

AC7: Lefthook pre-commit / pre-push pass cleanly with the `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]` shape; commit message marks the RED phase explicitly (e.g. `test(control-plane): RED — regression for subtree_control delegation`).

quality_gates: Compile-only on macOS via `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests --no-run`.

Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests` — new regression test passes as `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]`; all other cgroup_isolation tests continue to pass.
Lefthook pre-commit / pre-push pass; commit message marks RED phase explicitly. NO `--no-verify`.

Verify production code unchanged: `git diff --stat HEAD -- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs` returns empty.
deliverables: One commit landing the new typed-error variants + the failing RED regression test (passing as `should_panic`).

production: crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/error.rs (or wherever `CgroupBootstrapError` already lives — extend with `SubtreeControlBusy` and `SubtreeControlWriteFailed` variants; introduce the enum if absent, following the precedent of existing typed errors in the crate)
tests: crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/alloc_scope_has_writable_cpu_weight_and_memory_max.rs (new RED-scaffold regression test, ~150 lines including the AllocCleanup guard registration and tracing-capture assertion)

implementation_notes: Look at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/server_enrols_in_slice.rs` and `idempotent_slice_creation.rs` as the closest precedents for `#[serial(cgroup)]` shape and real-/sys/fs/cgroup test bodies in this crate.

AllocCleanup precedent: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/cleanup.rs::AllocCleanup`. Re-use it directly if visibility allows (likely `pub(crate)` accessible from sibling integration test modules); otherwise duplicate the small shape into the new test file with a comment cross-referencing the original.

RED scaffold shape (per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Test-side scaffolds'): `#[tokio::test]` + `#[serial(cgroup)]` + `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]`, with `panic!("Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold (subtree_control delegation regression)");` as the FIRST line of the body. The remaining body (the actual test logic) follows the panic so it compiles cleanly but is unreachable in this commit. When 01-02 lands the production fix, removing the `should_panic` attribute and the `panic!` line in one diff transitions RED → GREEN.

Tracing capture (USER-RESOLVED 2026-05-09 in /nw-deliver Phase 2 Q2 follow-up): use the custom `tracing::Layer` pattern at `crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/veth_attach.rs:307-310` (and the `CapturedEvent` / `CaptureLayer` shape at lines 339-381 of the same file). The rationale is documented inline at line 307: `Captured via a custom tracing::Layer rather than tracing-test, because we need to assert on the *event name*`. Copy the shape adjacent to the new test (small enough to inline; do NOT promote into a shared helper unless a third call site appears). Do NOT introduce `tracing-test` as a workspace dev-dep — the project has deliberately chosen the custom-Layer pattern.

DO NOT touch `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs`, `crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs`, or `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs` in this step. The whole point of the RED phase is that production code stays buggy and the new test demonstrates the bug-shaped contract that 01-02 will satisfy.

Conventional commit: `test(control-plane): RED — regression for subtree_control delegation` with body explaining the RED-scaffold state per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'RED scaffolds' template. Lefthook should pass — the `#[should_panic]` shape is hook-compatible per the same section.

steps.name: GREEN — implement subtree_control delegation in both inits + wire worker init
steps.description: Implement Production Fix #1 + #2 from the RCA. In `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs::create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at`, write `+cpu +memory +io +pids\n` to `overdrive.slice/cgroup.subtree_control` AFTER `mkdir overdrive.slice` and BEFORE creating `control-plane.slice` underneath OR enrolling the server PID — order is load-bearing per RCA § 'Production fix #1' (a process in `cgroup.procs` makes the kernel return EBUSY on parent `subtree_control` writes). In `crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs`, add `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(cgroup_root)` that `mkdir -p overdrive.slice/workloads.slice` then writes `+cpu +memory +io +pids\n` to `overdrive.slice/workloads.slice/cgroup.subtree_control` (same order invariant — enable controllers before any `alloc-*.scope` is created). Both inits surface failures via the typed-error variants from 01-01 (`SubtreeControlBusy` on EBUSY, `SubtreeControlWriteFailed` on any other I/O error) — NOT absorbed into a generic `io::Error`. Both inits are idempotent: a second call on already-enabled subtree_control is a no-op (the kernel re-confirms enablement). Wire the new worker init into the production bootstrap path at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:436` (or wherever `ExecDriver` is constructed for the production `cgroup_root`) — call it alongside the existing `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` call, before the convergence loop accepts any allocations. Remove the RED scaffold from the regression test landed in 01-01: drop the `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]` attribute and delete the `panic!` line. The test transitions RED → GREEN within this commit.
criteria: AC1: `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` (in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs`) writes `+cpu +memory +io +pids\n` to `<cgroup_root>/overdrive.slice/cgroup.subtree_control` AFTER `mkdir -p overdrive.slice` and BEFORE creating `control-plane.slice` underneath OR enrolling the server PID. Order verified by source-line ordering in the function body and by an idempotency test that calls the function twice in succession.

AC2: `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(cgroup_root)` exists in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs` as a public function. It performs `mkdir -p overdrive.slice/workloads.slice` (idempotent) then writes `+cpu +memory +io +pids\n` to `overdrive.slice/workloads.slice/cgroup.subtree_control` (also idempotent on re-enable). Per RCA § 'Production fix #2' ¶3, `+pids` and `+io` may also be enabled here for forward compatibility — crafter's call; document the rationale in the function rustdoc either way.

AC3: Both inits surface failures via discrete typed-error variants from 01-01: `SubtreeControlBusy` when the kernel returns EBUSY (use `io::Error::raw_os_error() == Some(libc::EBUSY)` to discriminate), `SubtreeControlWriteFailed` for any other I/O error. NOT absorbed into a generic `io::Error`. Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Distinct failure modes get distinct error variants'.

AC4: Both inits are idempotent under repeated boot. Crafter adds at least one test case (in `cgroup_isolation/`) that calls each init twice in succession on the same `cgroup_root` and asserts both calls return Ok with no observable side effects (the second call is a no-op write that the kernel accepts).

AC5: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:436` (or wherever `ExecDriver` is constructed for the production `cgroup_root`) calls `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(cgroup_root)` alongside the existing `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` call, before the convergence loop accepts any allocations. The two calls happen in either order — the worker init does not depend on the control-plane init being completed first (different slice paths).

AC6: The regression test from 01-01 (`alloc_scope_has_writable_cpu_weight_and_memory_max.rs`) loses its `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]` attribute and its leading `panic!("Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold ...")` line in this commit; the test now exercises the real production path and transitions RED → GREEN. The companion log-capture test from 01-01 also transitions GREEN.

AC7: Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests`: the regression test passes (asserts `cpu.weight=200`, `memory.max=134217728`, no WARN log line); all other `cgroup_isolation/` tests continue to pass. Workspace gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`: green.

AC8: Mutation gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs` ≥80% kill rate; `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` records `status="pass"`. Mutations on the new write paths (e.g. byte-content of the `+cpu +memory +io +pids` string, ordering of mkdir-vs-write, EBUSY-vs-other-error discrimination) MUST be killed by the new tests.

AC9: Conventional commit `fix(control-plane,worker): delegate cgroup subtree_control to overdrive.slice and workloads.slice` with body referencing the RCA, ADR-0026 D9 context, and the load-bearing write-order invariant.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` (in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs`) writes `+cpu +memory +io +pids\n` to `<cgroup_root>/overdrive.slice/cgroup.subtree_control` AFTER `mkdir -p overdrive.slice` and BEFORE creating `control-plane.slice` underneath OR enrolling the server PID. Order verified by source-line ordering in the function body and by an idempotency test that calls the function twice in succession.

AC2: `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(cgroup_root)` exists in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs` as a public function. It performs `mkdir -p overdrive.slice/workloads.slice` (idempotent) then writes `+cpu +memory +io +pids\n` to `overdrive.slice/workloads.slice/cgroup.subtree_control` (also idempotent on re-enable). Per RCA § 'Production fix #2' ¶3, `+pids` and `+io` may also be enabled here for forward compatibility — crafter's call; document the rationale in the function rustdoc either way.

AC3: Both inits surface failures via discrete typed-error variants from 01-01: `SubtreeControlBusy` when the kernel returns EBUSY (use `io::Error::raw_os_error() == Some(libc::EBUSY)` to discriminate), `SubtreeControlWriteFailed` for any other I/O error. NOT absorbed into a generic `io::Error`. Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Distinct failure modes get distinct error variants'.

AC4: Both inits are idempotent under repeated boot. Crafter adds at least one test case (in `cgroup_isolation/`) that calls each init twice in succession on the same `cgroup_root` and asserts both calls return Ok with no observable side effects (the second call is a no-op write that the kernel accepts).

AC5: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs:436` (or wherever `ExecDriver` is constructed for the production `cgroup_root`) calls `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(cgroup_root)` alongside the existing `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` call, before the convergence loop accepts any allocations. The two calls happen in either order — the worker init does not depend on the control-plane init being completed first (different slice paths).

AC6: The regression test from 01-01 (`alloc_scope_has_writable_cpu_weight_and_memory_max.rs`) loses its `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]` attribute and its leading `panic!("Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold ...")` line in this commit; the test now exercises the real production path and transitions RED → GREEN. The companion log-capture test from 01-01 also transitions GREEN.

AC7: Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests`: the regression test passes (asserts `cpu.weight=200`, `memory.max=134217728`, no WARN log line); all other `cgroup_isolation/` tests continue to pass. Workspace gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`: green.

AC8: Mutation gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs` ≥80% kill rate; `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` records `status="pass"`. Mutations on the new write paths (e.g. byte-content of the `+cpu +memory +io +pids` string, ordering of mkdir-vs-write, EBUSY-vs-other-error discrimination) MUST be killed by the new tests.

AC9: Conventional commit `fix(control-plane,worker): delegate cgroup subtree_control to overdrive.slice and workloads.slice` with body referencing the RCA, ADR-0026 D9 context, and the load-bearing write-order invariant.
quality_gates: Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests`: regression test from 01-01 transitions RED → GREEN; all other cgroup_isolation tests continue to pass.

Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker --features integration-tests`: existing exec_driver tests continue to pass (they still use TempDir at this point — Phase 02 migrates them).
Whole-workspace gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`: green.

Mutation gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs`: kill rate ≥80%, summary file `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` records `status="pass"`.
Idempotency verification: the crafter's tests (added or extended in this step) must boot each init twice and assert both calls return Ok with no side effects on the second call.
Conventional commit message: `fix(control-plane,worker): delegate cgroup subtree_control to overdrive.slice and workloads.slice`.
deliverables: One commit completing the production fix (both inits + wiring) and transitioning the regression test RED → GREEN.
Mutation gate pass artefact at `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` recording `status="pass"`.

production: crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs (extend `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at`: insert subtree_control write between the existing `mkdir overdrive.slice` and the existing `mkdir -p control-plane.slice` / `cgroup.procs` enrol; wire EBUSY discrimination via `raw_os_error() == Some(libc::EBUSY)`; route errors through the new typed variants from 01-01; add rustdoc on the load-bearing order invariant)

crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs (add public `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(cgroup_root)` that mkdir-then-writes; same EBUSY discrimination and typed-error routing; rustdoc names the order invariant and the idempotency contract)

crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs (call `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` alongside the existing `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` call at the bootstrap site near line 436; both calls complete before the convergence loop accepts allocations)
tests: crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/alloc_scope_has_writable_cpu_weight_and_memory_max.rs (drop `#[should_panic]` + leading `panic!`; the body remains as written in 01-01 and now exercises the real production path)

implementation_notes: Write-shape convention: per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'No blocking std::fs::* inside async fn', the bootstrap inits are likely sync (called once at startup, not per-tick). Use `std::fs::write` here — the call site is a sync init function called from `main()`-shaped code, not an async hot path. Verify by reading the existing `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` signature and matching it.

The trailing `
` matters: cgroupfs accepts both `+cpu` and `+cpu
`, but the kernel's documented shape is one controller per line OR space-separated controllers + trailing newline. Use `+cpu +memory +io +pids
` (space-separated, single trailing newline) to match the cgroup-v2 admin-guide shape. Same for `+cpu +memory +io +pids
` in the worker init.

EBUSY discrimination: `match err.raw_os_error() { Some(libc::EBUSY) => Err(SubtreeControlBusy { source: err }), _ => Err(SubtreeControlWriteFailed { source: err }) }`. The `libc` crate is already a workspace dep (verify via `grep '^libc' crates/overdrive-control-plane/Cargo.toml crates/overdrive-worker/Cargo.toml`); if not, surface to user before adding.

Idempotency under re-boot: the kernel accepts a second `+cpu +memory +io +pids` write on an already-enabled subtree_control as a no-op. The crafter's idempotency test calls the init function twice in succession on the same `cgroup_root` (which on Lima is `/sys/fs/cgroup`) and asserts both Ok. Use `#[serial(cgroup)]` on the idempotency test too.

Wiring at lib.rs:436: the line number is the RCA's reference at the time of writing; the crafter should grep for the existing `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at` call site rather than blindly trust the line number (file evolves). Place the new `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` call adjacent to it; both must complete before the convergence loop spawns. Order between the two does not matter (different slice paths, no shared invariant).

Regression test transition: replace the file's content from the RED scaffold shape (with `#[should_panic]` + leading `panic!`) to the GREEN shape (without those two attributes/lines, body intact). The body itself was already written in 01-01 — this commit just unblocks it.

Mutation gate considerations: the new write paths introduce mutable surface area in (a) the byte content of the controllers string, (b) the call ordering (mkdir-then-write vs write-then-mkdir, which the EBUSY path catches), (c) the EBUSY-vs-other-error discrimination, (d) the idempotency contract. The new regression test + idempotency test together should reach ≥80% kill rate; if a mutation survives, add a targeted test to kill it (do NOT skip via `// mutants: skip` without explicit rationale per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Mutation testing → Rules').

Post-mutation: cargo-mutants restores the working tree itself per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'No routine post-run cleanup'. Do NOT add `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation run; the destructive-git-ops hook blocks it anyway and the tool's own cleanup is sufficient.

phases.name: Broad-scope cleanup — migrate exec_driver tests off TempDir + delete dead code
user_stories: As a maintainer of the worker crate, the exec_driver integration tests exercise the real cgroup-v2 semantics (subtree_control delegation, controller-file existence) instead of a TempDir-based shadow that masked the subtree_control bug — so the next bug of this class fails the test bar pre-merge instead of shipping silently to production.

As a maintainer reading `cgroup_manager.rs`, the production code does not carry an unreachable ENOTEMPTY fallback branch and a `is_dir_not_empty` helper that exist only for the deleted TempDir test scaffolding.
phases.description: Mechanical broad-scope cleanup. 02-01 migrates all 10 `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/*.rs` files off `tempfile::TempDir` to real `/sys/fs/cgroup` via the `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` helper landed in 01-02. Each test boots the helper, registers an `AllocCleanup`-style guard, runs through `cargo xtask lima run --` (sudo by default per `.claude/rules/testing.md`), and carries `#[serial(cgroup)]`. Single-cut migration per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Single-cut migrations in greenfield' — TempDir scaffolding is replaced wholesale, no parallel old-path retention. 02-02 deletes the now-unreachable `tempdir-as-cgroupfs` ENOTEMPTY fallback in `remove_workload_scope` and the `is_dir_not_empty` helper (verified via grep that no other callers remain), updates docstrings on `create_workload_scope`, `remove_workload_scope`, `ExecDriver::build_command` etc. that still reference the TempDir rationale, and deletes any test that exists ONLY to cover the deleted branch (per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Deletion discipline').

steps.name: Migrate 10 exec_driver tests off TempDir to real /sys/fs/cgroup
steps.description: Rewrite all 10 files under `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/` to use real `/sys/fs/cgroup` instead of `tempfile::TempDir`. Each test: (1) calls `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(Path::new("/sys/fs/cgroup"))` at the top of the body (idempotent — safe across tests); (2) uses a unique `AllocationId` (preserving the existing per-test names like `alloc-resource-enforcement`, `alloc-warn-ok-0`, `alloc-create-0`, etc.); (3) registers an `AllocCleanup`-style RAII guard for that alloc scope so `cgroup.kill` + `rmdir` fire on Drop (panic OR clean return); (4) carries `#[serial(cgroup)]` to serialise within the binary; (5) constructs `ExecDriver::new(Path::new("/sys/fs/cgroup").to_path_buf(), ...)` instead of a tempdir-based root. The `force_limit_write_failure` injection seam at `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs:291-296` continues to fire synthetic EACCES regardless of the underlying filesystem — verify `limit_write_failure_warns.rs` continues to pass after the migration. Single-cut: TempDir imports come out, the new `/sys/fs/cgroup` shape goes in, no `#[cfg(...)]` gating between old and new paths. Files: `cgroup_procs.rs`, `limit_write_failure_warns.rs`, `live_map_bounded.rs`, `missing_binary.rs`, `resize_updates_limits.rs`, `resource_enforcement.rs`, `start_and_running.rs`, `stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs`, `stop_pid_none_handle_delivers_sigterm.rs`, `stop_with_grace.rs`. Conventional commit `test(worker): migrate exec_driver tests off TempDir to real /sys/fs/cgroup`.
criteria: AC1: All 10 listed files under `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/` no longer import `tempfile::TempDir` (verify via `grep -l 'tempfile::TempDir' crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/` returns empty after the commit). The `tempfile` dev-dep stays in the worker crate's `Cargo.toml` only if other tests still use it; otherwise it is removed in this commit (verify via `grep -r 'tempfile' crates/overdrive-worker/`).

AC2: Every migrated test calls `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(Path::new("/sys/fs/cgroup"))` (or equivalent — the bootstrap helper from 01-02) at the top of its body before constructing `ExecDriver`. The call is idempotent so cross-test contamination is impossible.

AC3: Every migrated test carries `#[serial(cgroup)]` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Tests that mutate process-global state'. Tests that already mutate other process-global state (e.g. env vars) carry both `#[serial(env)]` and `#[serial(cgroup)]` (serial_test composes group keys).

AC4: Every migrated test that creates an alloc scope under real `/sys/fs/cgroup` registers an `AllocCleanup`-style RAII guard before the alloc is created. The guard's `Drop` impl fires `cgroup.kill` then `rmdir` so leaked workloads do not survive the test process exit (per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Leaked workload cgroups across runs').

AC5: `limit_write_failure_warns.rs` continues to assert the warn-and-continue path correctly after the migration. The `force_limit_write_failure` injection seam at `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs:291-296` is UNTOUCHED in this commit; the test simply boots against real cgroupfs and the seam fires synthetic EACCES the same way it did under TempDir.

AC6: Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker --features integration-tests`: all 10 migrated tests pass. Whole-workspace gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`: green. Both before and after the run, the leftover-cgroup detection one-liner (`.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Leaked workload cgroups across runs' → 'Detection') returns no surprise stale alloc-*.scope directories.

AC7: Production source files `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` and `crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs` are UNMODIFIED in this commit (verify via `git diff --stat HEAD --` — only test files and possibly `Cargo.toml` (for tempfile removal) appear in the diff). Dead-code removal of the ENOTEMPTY fallback ships in 02-02, not here.

AC8: Conventional commit `test(worker): migrate exec_driver tests off TempDir to real /sys/fs/cgroup` with body explaining the masking failure mode the migration closes (RCA § D), the `#[serial(cgroup)]` + `AllocCleanup` discipline applied, and the cross-reference to 01-02's `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` helper.

The `tempfile` dev-dep stays in the worker crate's `Cargo.toml` only if other tests still use it; otherwise it is removed in this commit (verify via `grep -r 'tempfile' crates/overdrive-worker/`).

The `force_limit_write_failure` injection seam at `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs:291-296` is UNTOUCHED in this commit; the test simply boots against real cgroupfs and the seam fires synthetic EACCES the same way it did under TempDir.

AC6: Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker --features integration-tests`: all 10 migrated tests pass. Whole-workspace gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`: green. AC7: Production source files `crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs` and `crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs` are UNMODIFIED in this commit (verify via `git diff --stat HEAD --` — only test files and possibly `Cargo.toml` (for tempfile removal) appear in the diff). Dead-code removal of the ENOTEMPTY fallback ships in 02-02, not here.

AC8: Conventional commit `test(worker): migrate exec_driver tests off TempDir to real /sys/fs/cgroup` with body explaining the masking failure mode the migration closes (RCA § D), the `#[serial(cgroup)]` + `AllocCleanup` discipline applied, and the cross-reference to 01-02's `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` helper.
quality_gates: Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker --features integration-tests`: all 10 migrated exec_driver tests pass.
Whole-workspace gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`: green.
Leftover-cgroup detection (`.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Leaked workload cgroups across runs' → 'Detection' one-liner) returns no surprise stale alloc-*.scope directories after the test run.
Mutation gate is SKIPPED for this step (test-only, no production logic change). Per-PR final gate covers anything missed.
Production source unchanged: `git diff --stat HEAD -- crates/overdrive-worker/src/` returns empty.
Conventional commit message: `test(worker): migrate exec_driver tests off TempDir to real /sys/fs/cgroup`.
deliverables: One commit (or one commit per file at crafter's discretion) migrating all 10 exec_driver tests to real `/sys/fs/cgroup` with `#[serial(cgroup)]` and `AllocCleanup` discipline.

tests: crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/limit_write_failure_warns.rs (TempDir → /sys/fs/cgroup; force_limit_write_failure seam continues to fire synthetic EACCES)
crates/overdrive-worker/Cargo.toml (drop `tempfile` dev-dep IFF no other tests in the crate still use it; verify via `grep -r 'tempfile' crates/overdrive-worker/`)

implementation_notes: AllocCleanup precedent: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/job_lifecycle/cleanup.rs::AllocCleanup` is the canonical shape. If visibility allows reuse from sibling integration test modules in the worker crate, prefer reuse with a `pub(crate)` accessor or via a small shared test helper module; otherwise duplicate the small Drop-impl shape into a `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/cleanup.rs` file with a comment cross-referencing the original.

Per-test alloc IDs are already unique (`alloc-resource-enforcement`, `alloc-warn-ok-0`, `alloc-create-0`, etc.) — preserve them, do NOT renumber. Cross-test isolation derives from (a) the unique alloc ID, (b) the per-test AllocCleanup guard, (c) `#[serial(cgroup)]` serialisation. `overdrive.slice` and `workloads.slice` themselves stay across tests (idempotent across runs).

Migration shape per file: replace `let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap(); let cgroup_root = tmp.path();` with `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers(Path::new("/sys/fs/cgroup")).expect("workloads.slice bootstrap"); let cgroup_root = Path::new("/sys/fs/cgroup");`. Drop the `let _tmp = tmp;` lifetime extender. Add `let _cleanup = AllocCleanup::register(cgroup_root.to_path_buf(), alloc_id.clone());` immediately after the alloc ID is constructed.

`#[serial(cgroup)]` placement: directly above each `#[tokio::test]` (or `#[test]`) attribute. Tests that already carry `#[serial(env)]` add `#[serial(cgroup)]` as a sibling — serial_test composes group keys correctly per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Rules' for serial_test.

limit_write_failure_warns.rs: the `force_limit_write_failure` seam at `driver.rs:291-296` is filesystem-agnostic — it short-circuits the limit-write call with a synthetic EACCES regardless of whether the underlying path is on tmpfs or cgroupfs. After the migration, the test should still observe the WARN log line `cgroup resource-limit write failed`. If it doesn't, the seam is broken — surface as a blocker, do NOT paper over.

tempfile removal: only drop `tempfile.workspace = true` from `crates/overdrive-worker/Cargo.toml` if NO other test in the crate still uses it. Verify with `grep -r 'tempfile\|TempDir' crates/overdrive-worker/` and inspect every hit before dropping the dep. If unrelated tests still need it, leave the dep alone.

Pre-test cleanup discipline: per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § 'Leaked workload cgroups across runs', a failed prior run may leave stale alloc-*.scope directories. The crafter should run the cleanup one-liner from that section before the first test run to clear any pre-existing leaks; the AllocCleanup guards prevent new leaks from this run.

Single-cut per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Single-cut migrations in greenfield': do NOT introduce a `#[cfg(feature = "real-cgroupfs")]` toggle to keep the TempDir path alive in parallel. Replace wholesale; one cohesive commit (or one per file).

steps.name: Delete dead tempdir-as-cgroupfs fallback + helper + stale docstrings
steps.description: Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Deletion discipline': now that 02-01 has migrated every exec_driver test off TempDir, the `tempdir-as-cgroupfs` ENOTEMPTY fallback branch in `remove_workload_scope` (`crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs:268-...`) is unreachable in production AND in tests. Delete the branch. Then verify via `grep -rn 'is_dir_not_empty' crates/` that the `is_dir_not_empty` helper has no other callers; if so, delete it too. If a test exists ONLY to cover the deleted branch (use `git log -p` and the migration diff from 02-01 to identify), delete the test in this same commit per the deletion discipline. Update docstrings on `create_workload_scope`, `remove_workload_scope`, `ExecDriver::build_command`, and any other production code/tests that reference the TempDir scaffolding rationale (search via `grep -rn 'TempDir\|tempfile' crates/overdrive-worker/src/` and `grep -rn 'tempdir' crates/overdrive-worker/src/`). Conventional commit `chore(worker): remove dead tempdir-as-cgroupfs fallback now that exec_driver tests use real cgroupfs`.
criteria: AC1: The `tempdir-as-cgroupfs` ENOTEMPTY fallback branch in `remove_workload_scope` (`crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs:268-...`) is deleted. The function's Ok-path is now the single happy path: `rmdir` on the scope directory, with the only error surface being the kernel's response to a non-empty / non-existent / busy cgroup (typed via the existing error variants).

AC2: `is_dir_not_empty` helper is deleted IFF `grep -rn 'is_dir_not_empty' crates/` returns no other callers after the AC1 deletion. If other callers exist, document them in the commit message and leave the helper in place (with a docstring update naming the surviving callers).

AC3: Any test that exists ONLY to cover the deleted ENOTEMPTY fallback branch is deleted in the same commit per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Deletion discipline'. The crafter identifies such tests via `git grep -l 'ENOTEMPTY\|is_dir_not_empty\|remove_dir_all' crates/overdrive-worker/tests/` and inspects each match. A test that exercises additional behaviour beyond the fallback stays (with the fallback-specific assertions removed).

AC4: Docstrings on `create_workload_scope`, `remove_workload_scope`, `ExecDriver::build_command`, and any other production functions in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/` that reference the TempDir scaffolding rationale (verify via `grep -rn 'TempDir\|tempdir\|tempfile\|fake cgroup' crates/overdrive-worker/src/`) are updated to remove the stale TempDir context. Replace with documentation describing the real-cgroupfs contract these functions honor.

AC5: `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` passes (any clippy warnings surfaced by the deletion are fixed in-scope per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer`, NOT classified as deferrals).
AC6: Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`: all tests pass (the migrated exec_driver tests from 02-01 + every other crate's tests).

AC7: Mutation gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs` ≥80% kill rate; `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` records `status="pass"`. The deletion reduces mutable surface area; surviving mutations on the simplified `remove_workload_scope` Ok-path must be killed by the migrated 02-01 tests (which now exercise it for real).

AC8: Conventional commit `chore(worker): remove dead tempdir-as-cgroupfs fallback now that exec_driver tests use real cgroupfs` with body naming the deleted symbols (function/branch/helper/test names) and citing `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Deletion discipline'.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: The `tempdir-as-cgroupfs` ENOTEMPTY fallback branch in `remove_workload_scope` (`crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs:268-...`) is deleted. The function's Ok-path is now the single happy path: `rmdir` on the scope directory, with the only error surface being the kernel's response to a non-empty / non-existent / busy cgroup (typed via the existing error variants).

AC2: `is_dir_not_empty` helper is deleted IFF `grep -rn 'is_dir_not_empty' crates/` returns no other callers after the AC1 deletion. If other callers exist, document them in the commit message and leave the helper in place (with a docstring update naming the surviving callers).

AC3: Any test that exists ONLY to cover the deleted ENOTEMPTY fallback branch is deleted in the same commit per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Deletion discipline'. The crafter identifies such tests via `git grep -l 'ENOTEMPTY\|is_dir_not_empty\|remove_dir_all' crates/overdrive-worker/tests/` and inspects each match. A test that exercises additional behaviour beyond the fallback stays (with the fallback-specific assertions removed).

AC4: Docstrings on `create_workload_scope`, `remove_workload_scope`, `ExecDriver::build_command`, and any other production functions in `crates/overdrive-worker/src/` that reference the TempDir scaffolding rationale (verify via `grep -rn 'TempDir\|tempdir\|tempfile\|fake cgroup' crates/overdrive-worker/src/`) are updated to remove the stale TempDir context. Replace with documentation describing the real-cgroupfs contract these functions honor.

AC5: `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` passes (any clippy warnings surfaced by the deletion are fixed in-scope per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer`, NOT classified as deferrals).
AC6: Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`: all tests pass (the migrated exec_driver tests from 02-01 + every other crate's tests).

AC7: Mutation gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs` ≥80% kill rate; `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` records `status="pass"`. The deletion reduces mutable surface area; surviving mutations on the simplified `remove_workload_scope` Ok-path must be killed by the migrated 02-01 tests (which now exercise it for real).

AC8: Conventional commit `chore(worker): remove dead tempdir-as-cgroupfs fallback now that exec_driver tests use real cgroupfs` with body naming the deleted symbols (function/branch/helper/test names) and citing `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Deletion discipline'.
quality_gates: `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings`: green. Any new warnings surfaced by the deletion are fixed in-scope, NOT deferred.
Linux runtime via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`: green.

Mutation gate via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-worker --file crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs`: kill rate ≥80%, `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` records `status="pass"`.

Grep verification: `grep -rn 'is_dir_not_empty' crates/` returns empty (or returns surviving callers documented in the commit body); `grep -rn 'TempDir\|tempdir\|tempfile\|fake cgroup' crates/overdrive-worker/src/` returns empty (only test files may still reference tempfile if it's still a dev-dep for unrelated reasons).
Conventional commit message: `chore(worker): remove dead tempdir-as-cgroupfs fallback now that exec_driver tests use real cgroupfs`.
deliverables: One commit deleting the ENOTEMPTY fallback branch, the `is_dir_not_empty` helper (if no other callers), any test that exists ONLY to cover the deleted branch, and updating stale docstrings.

production: crates/overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs (delete the ENOTEMPTY fallback branch in `remove_workload_scope` at lines 268-...; delete `is_dir_not_empty` helper if no other callers; update docstrings on `create_workload_scope` and `remove_workload_scope` to remove TempDir-rationale text)
crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs (update docstring on `ExecDriver::build_command` and any other functions that reference TempDir rationale; production logic UNCHANGED)
tests: Any test under `crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/exec_driver/` that exists ONLY to cover the deleted ENOTEMPTY fallback (crafter identifies via grep + inspection during execution; if found, deleted in this commit per deletion discipline; if none found, the tests/ implementation_scope entry is empty)

implementation_notes: Identification of dead-only tests: run `git grep -l 'ENOTEMPTY\|is_dir_not_empty\|remove_dir_all' crates/overdrive-worker/tests/` and inspect each match. A test like `remove_workload_scope_handles_enotempty_via_remove_dir_all.rs` (or similar) is dead-only — delete. A test like `start_and_running.rs` that incidentally calls `remove_workload_scope` as part of cleanup is NOT dead-only — it tests the happy path which still exists; leave the test alone, the deletion narrows the function's surface but does not break the happy path.

Helper deletion verification: after deleting `is_dir_not_empty` from `cgroup_manager.rs`, run `cargo check -p overdrive-worker --features integration-tests` (via Lima per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Compile-checking'). If the check fails with `cannot find function is_dir_not_empty`, there's a surviving caller — leave the helper in place and document the caller in the commit body. If the check passes, the deletion is sound.

Docstring sweep: search via `grep -rn 'TempDir\|tempdir\|tempfile\|fake cgroup\|cgroupfs root' crates/overdrive-worker/src/`. Each hit on a docstring or comment that references the TempDir rationale gets rewritten to describe the real-cgroupfs contract instead. Function-body comments that explain the ENOTEMPTY fallback go away with the branch.

Clippy fixes are in-scope per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer` and CLAUDE.md § 'Deferrals require GitHub issues'. If the deletion surfaces a `dead_code` warning on something else (e.g. a related helper that was only called by `is_dir_not_empty`), follow the chain — delete that too, per `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Deletion discipline' (`A 'dead_code' warning... is the signal that more code needs deleting, not that the existing code needs a gate or an allow.`).

Mutation gate: with the ENOTEMPTY fallback gone, `remove_workload_scope` has a smaller mutable surface. The migrated 02-01 tests exercise the function's happy path against real cgroupfs; they should kill mutations on the simplified body. If a mutation survives, add a targeted test that asserts on the specific behaviour the mutation would change.

Commit body shape: name the deleted symbols explicitly so a future reviewer reading the git log sees what went away. Cite `.claude/rules/development.md` § 'Deletion discipline' to set the precedent for the next deletion-shaped commit.

validation.notes: Roadmap quality gate (6 checks) PASS: (1) AC coupling — all ACs reference public surface (function names like `create_and_enrol_control_plane_slice_at`, `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers`, `remove_workload_scope`; public typed-error variants `SubtreeControlBusy`, `SubtreeControlWriteFailed`; observable file-system fixtures under `/sys/fs/cgroup`; observable interface files `cpu.weight`, `memory.max`; observable log line `cgroup resource-limit write failed`). No private-method coupling. (2) Decomposition ratio — 4 steps over 4 production files (`overdrive-control-plane/src/cgroup_manager.rs`, `overdrive-worker/src/cgroup_manager.rs`, `overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`, `overdrive-worker/src/driver.rs`) + 11 test files (10 migrated + 1 new regression): 0.27, well under 2.5. (3) Distinct shapes — 01-01 is RED scaffold (typed errors + failing test); 01-02 is GREEN cohesive fix (both inits + wiring); 02-01 is mechanical test migration; 02-02 is dead-code deletion + docstring sweep. No batchable repetition. (4) All four steps have non-empty `files_to_modify`. (5) Step IDs match `^\d{2}-\d{2}$`: 01-01, 01-02, 02-01, 02-02. (6) DISTILL linkage — no `distill/` directory exists for this bugfix (per the bugfix template at `fix-cgroup-preflight-scope-vs-slice`); the RCA at `docs/feature/fix-cgroup-subtree-control-delegation/bugfix-rca.md` plays that role. All four steps name a `test_file` and `scenario_name`. User-approved 2026-05-09 in /nw-bugfix Phase 2 (BROAD scope explicitly chosen).
